channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Samoa warns of 'highly probable' fuel spill after New Zealand navy ship caught fire and sank
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A fuel spill is “highly probable” after a New Zealand navy ship grounded, caught fire and sank off the coast of Samoa, the Pacific island nation's acting prime minister said late Sunday. All 75 people on board the HMNZS Manawanui were taken to safety on life boats.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey high school allegedly banned yellow ribbons honoring Israeli hostages: 'Deeply offensive'
item.description: Yellow ribbons meant to honor Israeli hostages abducted by Hamas were allegedly banned at a club fair, which critics called "deeply offensive" and "blatant antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tips, overtime, Social Security: A look at Donald Trump's no-tax pledges and what they might cost
item.description: Donald Trump has pledged to end taxes on everything from tips to Social Security and overtime pay if he’s elected to the White House again.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hurricane Milton strengthens into a Category 4. Florida prepares for evacuations and storm surge
item.description: Milton has rapidly strengthened into a Category 4 hurricane as Florida gears up for what could be its biggest evacuation in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin And Freida Frisaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Turning Point wants to revolutionize how Republicans turn out voters. Some are skeptical
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Turning Point’s representatives have made two things clear in meetings with state and local Republican leaders — Donald Trump has blessed their conservative organization to help lead his get-out-the-vote effort, and local party officials ought to use the group's new voter mobilizat
item.dc:creator: DAN MERICA and BRIAN SLODYSKO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US spends a record $17.9 billion on military aid to Israel since last Oct. 7
item.description: The United States has spent a record of at least $17.9 billion on military aid to Israel since the war in Gaza began and led to escalating conflict around the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New analysis suggests national debt could increase under Harris, but it would surge under Trump
item.description: No one is likely to be happy with the projected higher deficits laid out in a new analysis of Kamala Harris’ and Donald Trump’s economic plans.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fixed-rate or adjustable-rate mortgage? Here’s what you need to know
item.description: Fixed-rate mortgages are simpler, dependable and less-stressful. An ARM may also be better for those who don’t plan on being in a home for a long time.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Dodger Stadium fans toss balls and trash on field, interrupt Padres' 10-2 win that evens NLDS
item.description: Fans threw baseballs in the direction of San Diego left fielder Jurickson Profar and then tossed trash that caused a lengthy delay before the Padres beat the Los Angeles Dodgers 10-2 on Sunday night to even their NL Division Series at a game apiece.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: More Americans than ever rely on government help: Report
item.description: 53% of Americans draw at least a quarter of their income from government aid like Social Security and Medicare.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns scores 10 points in abbreviated preseason debut with New York Knicks
item.description: Jalen Brunson thought twice about going 1-on-2 in transition, instead dribbled back outside and flipped the ball behind him to Karl-Anthony Towns, who made a 3-pointer with a defender in his face.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending criminal probe of US woman's death
item.description: Swiss police arrested several people following the death of an unidentified 64-year-old woman from the U.S. Midwest who on Sept. 23 became the first person to use the device.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: See the 10 most – and least – valuable college majors: study
item.description: A new study from financial publisher Bankrate found that STEM college majors dominate the ranking of valuable fields of study – but money isn't the only consideration.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee nurse and his dog died trying to save a man from floods driven by Helene
item.description: “I’ve never had anyone risk their life for me,” David Boutin, the man Boone McCrary had set out to rescue said. “From what I hear that was the way he always been. He’s my guardian angel, that’s for sure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Here's what Spirit Halloween seeks in an 'ideal' retail location
item.description: Spirit Halloween made headlines earlier this week after firing back at "SNL" over a sketch that mocked the company's choice of retail locations.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricane Milton growing stronger as it approaches Florida: NHC
item.description: Milton has strengthed into a hurricane in the Gulf of Mexico, according to the National Hurricane Center.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for 5th consecutive year
item.description: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for the fifth year in a row, the retail giant recently announced in its holiday plans.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Can you stop calls and texts from political campaigns?
item.description: If you've received a political text or call recently, you may be wondering how to make it stop, or what you can do to prevent them in the future.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, Fox 5

item.title: Couple reunited after volunteer rescue pilot threatened with arrest in North Carolina
item.description: Jordan Seidhom, who decided to go help those impacted by Helene with his own helicopter on his own dime, was able to rescue Susan Coffey off a mountainside. But, before he could go back for her husband, Mike, and his own son and co-pilot, he was threatened with arrest.
item.dc:creator: Jody Barr, Fox 5

item.title: Eric Adams channels Trump as he ramps up revenge accusations against the Biden admin, expert says
item.description: Two New Yorkers on opposite sides of the political spectrum have a similar beef with the federal government — allegations of politically motivated prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security COLA raise: How much will recipients get in 2025?
item.description: The Social Security Administration is less than two weeks away from announcing its cost of living adjustment (COLA), the annual bump in recipients' payments to help them deal with inflation.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Keanu Reeves spins out at Indianapolis Motor Speedway in pro auto racing debut
item.description: Hollywood star Keanu Reeves made his professional auto racing debut on Saturday in an event in which “The Matrix” star spun out at famed Indianapolis Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Where do Trump, Harris stand on marijuana policy?
item.description: In a poll taken last year, roughly 70% of American adults said marijuana should be legal — but where do the presidential candidates stand?
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Child entrepreneur opens pizza shop with Caribbean twist in New York City
item.description: A young entrepreneur is bringing even more Caribbean flavor to New York City, opening a pizza shop with a Caribbean twist at 11 years old.
item.dc:creator: Zhané Caldwell, KRON4

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: Few things are as universally reviled as a loud phone conversation infringing on your personal space. (Mosquitoes and taxes, aside.) Doing it on loudspeaker is even more incendiary.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: After the deluge, the lies: Misinformation and hoaxes about Helene cloud the recovery
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The facts emerging from Hurricane Helene's destruction are heartrending: Businesses and homes destroyed, whole communities nearly wiped out, hundreds of lives lost, hundreds of people missing.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Does a cumulus cloud really weigh a million pounds?
item.description: Just because something floats doesn't mean it is weightless. A great example is when oil is poured onto water. The oil will float on top of the water, but that doesn't mean it has no weight.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Bacca, Fox 5

item.title: A Texas execution is renewing calls for clemency. It's rarely granted
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man set to die this month is at the center of another push for clemency in the U.S., this time backed by several GOP lawmakers and bestselling author John Grisham, who say a father’s 2002 conviction for killing his infant daughter deserves a second look.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN and SUMMER BALLENTINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What renters should know about breaking a lease to buy a home
item.description: Renters can break their lease to purchase their own home but they should understand the ramifications of leaving early
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: Why are there still so many blackouts after major storms?
item.description: The high costs of infrastructure improvements likely have contributed to the blackouts. Experts say more can be done to prevent power outages, but it would require significant investments.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KRON4

item.title: North Carolina is distributing Benadryl and EpiPens as yellow jackets swarm from Helene flooding
item.description: Deadly flooding from Hurricane Helene in western North Carolina has also disrupted the underground nests of yellow jackets, bees and other insects, causing them to swarm and sting people struggling to recover from the storm.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What is Marburg virus? Outbreak of Ebola-like disease detected in yet another country
item.description: Health officials in Rwanda are dealing with the country’s first outbreak of the Marburg virus, an Ebola-like disease which, if left untreated, has a fatality rate of up to 88%.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: South Carolina sets Nov. 1 execution as state ramps up use of death chamber
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina's high court on Friday set a date of Nov. 1 to put to death a man who killed a store clerk a quarter-century ago, the second of an expected six executions in about six months as the state ramps up its use of capital punishment after a 13-year pause.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden student loan cancellation plan on hold again
item.description: The Biden administration’s student loan cancellation plan has been put on hold once again in Missouri after brief celebration from advocates this week when another judge ruled the plan could move forward.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Melania Trump says she supports abortion rights, putting her at odds with the GOP
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Melania Trump revealed her support for abortion rights Thursday ahead of the release of her upcoming memoir, exposing a stark contrast with her husband, Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump, on the crucial election issue.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Vance-Walz VP debate
item.description: Ohio Sen. JD Vance (R) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) met face-to-face in New York City on Tuesday in the first and likely only vice presidential debate of the election cycle, a high-stakes moment that comes a little more than a month before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WFLA, NBC
